w:r.w:t: This document specifies the complete Game Room system for the Graviton Social Gaming Portal. It covers the data model, room lifecycle, host flow, guest flow, real-time behaviour, coin escrow logic, and UI requirements. Build exactly what is described here — no feature should be assumed or added beyond this spec.
A game room is a temporary session, not a permanent space. It is created by a host, exists only while the host is present, and dissolves completely when the session ends. No host = no room.
1.1 What the game room system does
Allows a user (the host) to create a private, temporary room for playing a game with friends
Supports coin stakes — players can put digital currency on the line before a match
Uses real-time presence so all players see each other's status live in the lobby
Integrates with the admin portal, which configures the available modes and stakes options
Dissolves automatically after the game ends or the host cancels
There are no permanent rooms or persistent group spaces
There is no Public Rooms feature — do not build this
A user cannot host more than one active room simultaneously
Guests cannot transfer host privileges — the room owner is always the creator
All game room data lives in Supabase. The following tables and columns are required.
Room display name set by host
References auth.users. Deleting the host dissolves the room.
References games table. Must always be set — never null after creation.
Coin amount per player. 0 = free play.
Default 4. Configurable by admin per game.
Null until dissolved. Set on cancel or game end.
One row per player per room. Tracks lobby presence and readiness.
Default false. Reset to false if host changes game or stakes.
When the player entered the lobby.
Null until player leaves or is kicked.
Tracks pending invitations. Expires when the room dissolves.
Append-only ledger for all coin movements. Never update or delete rows.
The user whose balance is affected
The room this transaction belongs to
Every room passes through exactly four states. The status column in game_rooms tracks this.
Host is going through the creation wizard. Room exists in DB but is not visible to other users.
Host completes all wizard steps and confirms.
Lobby is live. Guests can join via invite. Host can still change game or stakes (resets all ready statuses).
Host clicks Start and all players are marked is_ready = true.
Game is in progress. Coins are escrowed. Room is locked — no new players can join.
Game ends (natural completion) or host force-closes.
Room is over. No further actions possible. Record is retained for audit but treated as inactive.
Reached from open (cancel) or live (game end / host disconnect).
The following events dissolve a room. Each must be handled explicitly in code.
No escrow has occurred. No coin movement needed.
Real-time toast: "Host ended the room." Redirected to /dashboard/gameroom.
Escrow released. Winner receives full pot. Losers' coins are not returned.
Result screen shown with final scores and wallet update.
60-second grace period. If host reconnects within 60s, room continues. After 60s: escrow is reversed (release_cancel), all players refunded, room dissolved.
Countdown banner: "Host disconnected — room closing in Xs." Redirect after countdown.
Host cancels from live game (force quit)
Escrow reversed immediately. All players refunded via release_cancel transactions.
Toast: "Host ended the game early. Coins returned." Redirect to /dashboard/gameroom.

The host creates a room through a 4-step wizard. Each step must be completed before advancing. The wizard creates the room record in the DB at step 1 with status = forming. Status updates to open when the host enters the lobby.
Each screen has exactly one job. Do not combine steps. Do not show the full form at once.
Host selects which type of room to create. This drives all downstream options.
friend_room: Private, flexible stakes, no leaderboard impact online_ranked: Public matchmaking, affects global leaderboard practice: Solo/bot opponents, no coins, no rankings
Mode options are configured in the admin portal. Fetch from admin config — do not hardcode.
A mode must be selected before Next is enabled.
Insert row into game_rooms with host_id, mode, status = forming.
Host selects which game the room will play.
Fetch from games table where is_published = true. Render as a grid of cards showing game name, category tag, and thumbnail.
A game must be selected before Next is enabled. game_id must never be null when room status leaves forming.
Host sets the coin amount each player must commit.
Fetch available stake amounts from admin config for the selected mode. Always include Free play (0 coins) as the first option.

A stake option must be selected before Next is enabled. Show the host's current coin balance. If stake amount exceeds balance, show a warning but still allow selection (guest may also be blocked at join time).
For practice mode, skip this step entirely — stakes are always 0.
Host invites friends into the room before entering the lobby.
Show only friends where presence status = online. Friends in another live game show as Busy and their invite button is disabled. Friends who are offline are not shown.

Tapping Invite on a friend: inserts a row into room_invites with status = pending. Sends a real-time notification to the invitee via Supabase real-time channel. Button changes to Invited with a checkmark. Host can un-invite before entering lobby.
Host can skip inviting and open the lobby immediately. The lobby stays open for latecomers.
Insert rows into room_invites for each invited friend.
Host is redirected here after Step 4. Status updated to open.
The lobby shows the room's current state in real-time. It must update without page refresh using Supabase real-time listeners on the room_players table.
Always visible at top of lobby
Shows: game name, stake amount, room mode
Game and stakes have inline 'change' links (see Section 4.2)
Shows all players currently in room_players for this room
Each row: avatar, display name, ready badge (Ready or Waiting)
Host is always shown first with a '(host)' label
Updates in real-time as guests join, leave, or toggle ready
Disabled until ALL players in room_players have is_ready = true
When enabled: label is 'Start game'. When disabled: label is 'Waiting for players'
On click: triggers coin escrow (see Section 6), updates room status to live, launches game for all players
On click: show inline confirmation modal (not a page navigation)
Confirmation text: 'End this room? All players will be removed and any pending invites will expire.'
Confirm action: set room status to dissolved, remove all room_players rows, expire all pending invites, redirect host to /dashboard/gameroom
4.2 Changing game or stakes from lobby
If the host changes the game or stakes after players have already marked ready, all players must be reset to is_ready = false automatically.

Change game: show an inline panel with the game list. On select, update game_rooms.game_id, reset all room_players.is_ready to false. Broadcast a real-time event to all connected clients. All guest screens show a banner: 'The host changed the game — please re-confirm your ready status.'
Change stakes: same behaviour as change game but for stake_amount.
The change panels are inline (within the lobby page). Do not navigate away from the lobby to change settings.
When a host invites a friend, the friend receives a real-time notification. This notification must appear without a page refresh, wherever the guest currently is in the app.
Room name, game name, stake amount, mode, host display name
Insert a row into room_players for the guest. Update room_invites.status to accepted. Redirect guest to /dashboard/gameroom/[roomId]/lobby.
Update room_invites.status to declined. Dismiss notification. Host is not actively notified of decline — they see the player count simply not increase.
If room status changes to dissolved while invite is pending, the notification is dismissed and invite status set to expired.
The guest lobby is the same route as the host lobby (/dashboard/gameroom/[roomId]/lobby) but renders differently based on whether the current user is the host.
No 'Start game' button. No 'change game/stakes' links. Shows 'Mark as ready' button instead.
Display a banner: 'Marking ready means you agree to [X] coins being escrowed when the host starts the game.' Only show if stake_amount > 0.
On click: set room_players.is_ready = true for the guest. Button changes to 'Unready'. Guest can toggle back to not ready before host starts.
If host changes game or stakes, guest's is_ready is reset to false server-side. Guest sees a banner explaining why. They must re-confirm.
Guest can leave the lobby at any time. Sets room_players.left_at timestamp. Host sees the slot free. Room continues — it does not dissolve because a guest left.
All player ready statuses update live without refresh.
Coins are escrowed at the moment the host clicks Start, not when players mark ready. If stake_amount = 0, skip all escrow logic entirely.
Verify all players in room_players have is_ready = true. If any are false, reject the Start action and show an error.
For each player in room_players, verify their coin balance >= stake_amount. If any player has insufficient funds, block Start and show which player(s) cannot afford the stake.
In a single Supabase transaction: deduct stake_amount from each player's coin balance. Insert a coin_transactions row for each player with type = escrow and amount = -stake_amount.
Broadcast real-time event to all clients to launch the game.
When the game ends, the game client reports the result to the server (winner user_id).
Calculate total pot: stake_amount x number of players in room_players.
Credit the full pot to the winner's coin balance.
Insert a coin_transactions row for the winner with type = release_win and amount = total pot.
Redirect all clients to the result screen.
Triggered by: host cancels from live game, or host disconnect grace period expires.
For each player in room_players: credit stake_amount back to their coin balance.
Insert a coin_transactions row for each player with type = release_cancel and amount = +stake_amount.
Broadcast real-time dissolve event to all clients.
This is the main game room page where users see their active room and pending invites.
Title: 'Game rooms'. Subtitle: 'Rooms dissolve when the session ends. No host, no room.' Create room button (top right).
Two tabs only: 'My room' and 'Invites'. No Public Rooms tab. The Invites tab shows a count badge if there are pending invites.
Shows the user's single active hosted room, or an empty state.
Status indicator: animated green dot for live, purple dot for open/waiting
Mode pill (Friend room / Online ranked / Practice)
Stakes pill (Free play or X coins)
Two buttons: 'Open lobby' (primary — navigates to lobby) and 'Cancel room' (danger — shows inline confirmation)
Text: 'No active room. Create a room to invite friends and start playing.'
If user already has an active room (status = open or live), the 'Create room' button is disabled with tooltip: 'You already have an active room. Cancel it before creating a new one.'
Shows all room_invites rows where invitee_id = current user and status = pending.
Stakes pill — prominently shown so user knows what they're committing to
Text: 'No pending invites. When a friend invites you to a room, it will appear here.'
All real-time functionality is built on Supabase Realtime. The following events must be broadcast and handled.
Update player list, show new avatar and Waiting badge
Update player's badge from Waiting to Ready or vice versa. Enable Start if all ready.
Remove player from list. Disable Start if it was enabled.
Show settings-changed banner on all guest screens. Reset all ready badges to Waiting.
All clients transition to game view simultaneously.
Room status set to dissolved for any reason
All clients redirected to /dashboard/gameroom. Show appropriate toast message.
New row in room_invites for this user
Show real-time invite notification anywhere in app.
All tables must have RLS enabled. The following policies define who can read and write each table.
SELECT: User can read a room if they are the host_id, OR they have a row in room_players for this room, OR they have a pending/accepted invite in room_invites for this room.
INSERT: Authenticated users only. User can only set themselves as host_id.
UPDATE: Only the host (host_id = auth.uid()) can update the room.
DELETE: Not allowed. Use status = dissolved instead.
SELECT: User can read rows where room_id matches a room they have access to (same rule as game_rooms SELECT).
INSERT: User can only insert a row for themselves (user_id = auth.uid()). Room must have status = open.
UPDATE: User can only update their own row (is_ready toggle). Host can update any row in their room.
SELECT: User can read rows where invitee_id = auth.uid() OR inviter_id = auth.uid().
INSERT: Only the room host can insert invites for their room.
UPDATE: Only the invitee can update status (to accepted or declined).
SELECT: User can only read rows where user_id = auth.uid().
INSERT: Only server-side functions (not client). All escrow logic runs in Supabase Edge Functions or database functions, never from the client.
UPDATE/DELETE: Not allowed. This is an append-only ledger.
The admin portal configures options that the game room system consumes. Do not hardcode any of the following values — always fetch from the admin config.
Available room modes (friend_room, online_ranked, practice) and whether each is enabled
Stake amount options per mode (e.g. friend_room: [0, 50, 200, 500])
Whether a mode affects the leaderboard (leaderboard_impact: boolean)
Published games available in the game picker (is_published = true in games table)
User tries to join a full room
If room_players count >= game's max_players, reject the join and show error: 'This room is full.'
User tries to join a live room
If room status = live, reject the join and show error: 'This game has already started.'
Player has insufficient coins at Start
Block the Start action. Show a message identifying which player(s) cannot cover the stake. Host must either lower the stakes or wait for the player to top up.
Host refreshes or closes the tab during lobby
Room stays open. Supabase presence channel detects host absence. After 60 seconds with no reconnection: dissolve the room, refund any escrowed coins, notify all guests.
Guest is reconnected to the same lobby. Their is_ready status is preserved from the DB.
If a user already has a row in room_players for this room, do not insert a new row. Re-subscribe them to the real-time channel and redirect to the lobby.
Main listing page — My room and Invites tabs
Wizard step 3: set stakes (skip for practice)
Live lobby — host and guest views (same route, different render)
Next.js (frontend) · Supabase (database, auth, real-time, RLS) · Supabase Edge Functions (coin transactions)
Public Rooms tab · permanent rooms · host transfer · multiple simultaneous hosted rooms per user · any feature not listed in this document
All coin escrow and release logic must run server-side in Supabase Edge Functions or database functions. Never trust the client for balance changes.
